Event: He abandons using "But I am already in my pajamas" as a catchphrase after the first two episodes
Explanation: Dropping an unmemorable catchphrase has mild benefit to him and no clear effect on others.

Event: He unintentionally reuses the abandoned catchphrase in Season 8
Explanation: Repeating the line has negligible effects beyond light humor for everyone.

Event: He goes to an inventors' competition with last year's death clock
Explanation: Bringing the same invention again mildly harms his prospects while entertaining or interesting others.

Event: After realizing he reused last year's invention, he tries to invent the same thing again
Explanation: His absent-minded attempt wastes his own effort while providing mild amusement to onlookers.

Event: He quickly creates the smell-o-scope to replace the repeated invention
Explanation: Creating a working new invention is a strong win for him and a notable benefit to others.

Event: He remembers he already built the smell-o-scope last year
Explanation: The realization undercuts the value of his “new” work, mostly just mildly embarrassing him.

Event: He fathers Igner at around 160 years old
Explanation: Despite the oddity, he gains a son and Igner exists; no clear net harm shown.

Event: He spends more time on personal obsessions than managing his company
Explanation: His neglect of management serves his whims while moderately disadvantaging the company and crew.

Event: He creates doomsday devices for no apparent reason
Explanation: He gains pride and capability but greatly endangers others by stockpiling apocalyptic weapons.

Event: He states there is no scientific consensus that life is important
Explanation: Reinforces his own worldview at the expense of others’ sense of safety and value.

Event: He admits he always knew he would have a hand in ending the world
Explanation: Accepting an apocalyptic role bolsters his self-image while implying severe potential harm.

Event: He takes pleasure in endangering his crew’s lives
Explanation: He gets entertainment while significantly increasing risk to the crew.

Event: He gets upset when he discovers he won’t have to kill Fry to save humanity
Explanation: He loses the grim satisfaction of sacrifice while showing disregard for Fry’s life.

Event: He genuinely regrets endangering the crew once they are dead
Explanation: His remorse hurts himself emotionally and helps no one, as they’re already dead.

Event: He goes on the front line to save the world when it’s threatened
Explanation: He risks himself to stop threats, benefiting both himself and everyone else.

Event: He builds an Angry Dome room specifically to vent his rage
Explanation: Channeling anger into a private space helps him decompress and shields others somewhat.

Event: He cheerfully sends the crew on very dangerous missions
Explanation: He advances his goals and curiosity by risking the crew’s lives.

Event: He previously sent other crews on similarly dangerous missions that killed them
Explanation: He benefited from repeated missions while causing multiple fatal outcomes.

Event: He refuses to bring the Planet Express ship up to government standards despite lawsuits
Explanation: He saves money/effort while endangering staff and exposing them to legal risk.

Event: He habitually introduces dangerous missions as “Good news, everyone!”
Explanation: He preserves his upbeat framing while misleading or downplaying real danger to others.

Event: He creates Atomic Supermen to face the Harlem Globetrotters using chronotons
Explanation: He gains a solution and scientific thrill while destabilizing spacetime.

Event: He immediately flies into a rage if someone asks to use the ship as a mere lift
Explanation: His overreaction protects his principle at the cost of crew morale.

Event: He immediately flies into a rage at non-scientific explanations
Explanation: It satisfies his ego while discouraging alternative perspectives.

Event: He invents the impossible ship engine that moves the universe around the ship
Explanation: The engine is a massive technological gain with large shared benefits.

Event: He becomes a strong advocate against robosexuality
Explanation: He gains political and emotional satisfaction while restricting others’ relationships.

Event: He delays action because “I am already in my pajamas”
Explanation: His laziness mildly hurts his own productivity and sometimes others depending on him.

Event: He creates a glow-in-the-dark nose machine that produces huge toxic waste
Explanation: He gains little lasting benefit while creating significant, unusable toxic byproducts.

Event: He nearly destroys the fabric of space-time by aging engineered basketball players
Explanation: A trivial sports aim nearly annihilates existence, with negligible real benefit to him.

Event: He nods along when Fry calls him an “amoral crackpot”
Explanation: Accepting an accurate label clarifies expectations and causes minimal harm.

Event: He expects to suffer eternal damnation when he dies
Explanation: This belief harms his own mental comfort without affecting others.

Event: He repeatedly sends the crew on missions of extreme danger while framing it as good news
Explanation: He advances exploration and business at a steep, often hidden risk to his employees.

Event: He screams that he is “off to Hell” when startled by a bright light
Explanation: Low-stakes overreaction is just comic panic with no meaningful impact.

Event: He frequently throws the crew under the bus when Planet Express is threatened
Explanation: He protects his ownership and position while sacrificing subordinates’ interests.

Event: At the stockholders’ meeting he appears confused and shows he doesn’t know why he’s there
Explanation: His senility harms his credibility and the company, giving no gain.

Event: He ignores instructions about time travel and nearly causes paradoxes
Explanation: His carelessness gives him adventure at substantial risk to everyone’s timeline.

Event: He shoots Hitler while time traveling
Explanation: Assassinating Hitler confers historical benefit while posing some risk to him.

Event: He leaves Mom over her evil plans
Explanation: He preserves his conscience and reduces alignment with her harmful schemes.

Event: He is reluctant to directly fire the crew after big screw-ups
Explanation: He keeps experienced employees, benefiting both himself and them.

Event: He promotes Bender to captain for lacking intuition or heart and snubs Fry
Explanation: A petty, ideology-driven decision undermines mission safety to indulge his bias.

Event: He attaches a shock collar to Leela to control her rage outbursts
Explanation: He gains control over a threat but inflicts involuntary harm and loss of autonomy on her.

Event: He stuns a fleeing Lovey Bear after decrying their suffering
Explanation: He compromises his stated sympathy to secure a contract and maintain the mission.

Event: He buys a corpse of a Zuban pharaoh intending to eat it
Explanation: He indulges his tastes while violating taboos and basic respect.

Event: He becomes giddy at the prospect of eating someone’s heart for courage
Explanation: He contemplates cannibalism for personal “gain” at serious potential harm to others.

Event: He leaves Mom the first time because she wanted his soft toy made fifteen feet tall
Explanation: Breaking with a partner over impractical expansion preserves his standards with minor shared cost.

Event: He repeatedly gets back together with Mom then leaves her again when recalling she’s evil
Explanation: This oscillation hurts both emotionally while providing only temporary gratification.

Event: He keeps several doomsday weapons lying around his lab
Explanation: He enjoys power and security but imposes constant risk on everyone else.

Event: He throws especially unstable doomsday weapons in the garbage
Explanation: Disposing of them unsafely keeps risk while removing his own possible utility.

Event: He walks around naked or half-naked in inappropriate contexts
Explanation: He enjoys comfort and exhibition while making others uncomfortable or endangered (tar scene).

Event: He goes into searing hot tar naked despite the doctor’s warning
Explanation: Ignoring safety advice strongly endangers himself for no real gain.

Event: He frequently, knowingly sends his crew on life-threatening adventures without warning
Explanation: He gets missions done while maximizing surprise risk to employees’ lives.

Event: He hires replacement crews before current crew deaths are confirmed
Explanation: He ensures business continuity at the cost of showing utter disregard for current staff.

Event: He created the modern robot, an ecological disaster
Explanation: He gains prestige and utility, but robotics as implemented severely harms the ecosystem.

Event: He tries to harvest Leela’s organs
Explanation: He seeks material benefit at extreme potential harm to her.

Event: He kills people for their stem cells
Explanation: He gains scientific resources while causing maximum harm to others.

Event: He is frequently cited for public nudity
Explanation: He prioritizes his comfort over public norms, mildly inconveniencing and offending others.

Event: He walks nude despite being told it’s unnecessary and unwise
Explanation: He exposes himself to legal and physical risk with virtually no benefit.

Event: He embraces being a Satanist / invokes Satan’s name
Explanation: It mildly hurts his reputation and offers negligible tangible benefit.

Event: He keeps at least one Martian skull as a mug and dismisses their complaints as whining
Explanation: He enjoys a trophy mug while disrespecting and dehumanizing a whole group.

Event: He asks Glurmo to tell the Grunka Lunkas that he hates them
Explanation: Expressing hatred gratifies him emotionally while directly harming their dignity.

Event: He sabotages Cubert’s newspaper business by kicking his sign
Explanation: His petty action undermines his son’s initiative, slightly hurting himself via lost potential.

Event: He spanks Amy’s bottom after her rebirth despite her being conscious
Explanation: He takes perverse pleasure while violating her personal boundaries.

Event: He goes along with Fry’s lie about being blind to ogle a naked Michelle clone
Explanation: He satisfies voyeurism at others’ expense and undermines trust.

Event: He acts as an eccentric mentor when he has a good idea
Explanation: His guidance improves mission outcomes and gives him satisfaction as a teacher.

Event: He genuinely loves and cares for Cubert as his son
Explanation: A strong positive relationship benefits both him and the child.

Event: He demands the crew not go after Space Bee honey due to prior crew deaths
Explanation: He tries to prevent repeating fatal mistakes, helping everyone survive.

Event: He sets his time machine to only go forward so Fry can’t repeat his incestuous accident
Explanation: This choice sacrifices backward travel options to protect Fry and history.

Event: He left Mom over her evil plans partly due to their impracticality
Explanation: Rejecting evil and unworkable ideas benefits him ethically and the world practically.

Event: He is careful and somewhat reluctant about when to actually punish the crew
Explanation: Moderating punishment supports morale while maintaining some discipline.

Event: He shoots at robotic Santa (Bender) forgetting Leela’s warning
Explanation: He indulges aggressive impulse while endangering a teammate due to poor memory.

Event: He uses a crotchety grandpa discount card and acts entitled in lines
Explanation: He extracts small financial and time benefits while inconveniencing others.

Event: He falls asleep while skiing and wakes with a bronze medal
Explanation: He unexpectedly gains a medal with little discernible harm to anyone else.

Event: He shamelessly embraces public nudity as comedic fanservice
Explanation: He indulges himself while grossing out or disturbing others around him.

Event: He almost singlehandedly kills a giant space yeti when younger
Explanation: Defeating a major threat enhances his reputation and saves others.

Event: He clones himself a son from a growth on his back
Explanation: He gains an heir and Cubert gains life and family, with only weirdness as a cost.

Event: He has Cubert celebrate “growth-scraping day” instead of a birthday
Explanation: The unconventional celebration sustains their bond with only mild social oddity.

Event: He tampers with Wernstrom’s cloning machine, corrupting Zapp’s DNA
Explanation: Petty sabotage leads to a disastrously incompetent officer whose mistakes cost many lives.
